intro-1.plaintext: To make your site editable with Hydra you load hydra.js in your frontend and configure it with your block types. Then you progressively add HTML annotations to enable visual editing features.

children.text: To make your site editable with Hydra you load hydra.js in your frontend and configure it with your block types. Then you progressively add HTML annotations to enable visual editing features.
Setting Up the Bridge

bridge-text.plaintext: Include hydra.js in your frontend and call initBridge() to create a two-way communication channel between Hydra and your frontend.

children.text: Include hydra.js in your frontend and call
initBridge()
to create a two-way communication channel between Hydra and your frontend. The bridge handles authentication, page navigation syncing, and block data transfer.
The
pageBlocksFields

array configures which blocks fields your page has. Each entry specifies the field name, a display title, and which block types are allowed. If you don't specify a 'blocks' field, one is automatically added with all non-restricted block types.
Multiple Page Blocks Fields

multi-text.plaintext: Pages can have multiple blocks fields for different areas like header, content and footer.

children.text: Pages can have multiple blocks fields for different areas (e.g., header, content, footer). Each shows as a separate section in the sidebar when no block is selected:
The Update Callback

callback-text.plaintext: Register onEditChange to receive live content updates as the user edits. Your frontend re-renders in realtime using the same data format as the Plone REST API.

children.text: Register
onEditChange
to receive live content updates as the user edits. Your frontend re-renders in realtime using the same data format as the Plone REST API.
Since the data structure is the same as returned by the Plone REST API, it's normally easy to re-render your page dynamically using the same code your frontend used to render the page initially.
With onEditChange registered, editors get:
Changes appear on the frontend as they type in the sidebar
Adding and removing blocks shows immediately in the preview
Drag and drop block reordering with live preview
A '+' icon on the frontend to add blocks after the current block
Note: For live updates you need a frontend deployed as SPA or hybrid rather than SSR or SSG. If your production deploy is SSG/SSR, deploy another edit-only preview frontend in SPA mode.
Custom Blocks

custom-text.plaintext: Define custom block types directly in your frontend configuration via voltoConfig.blocks.blocksConfig. No Volto plugin required.

children.text: Define custom block types directly in your frontend configuration via
voltoConfig.blocks.blocksConfig
. No Volto plugin deployment required. Each block type needs an id, title, and a blockSchema with its field properties.
Child block types (like 'slide' above) must be defined at the top level of blocksConfig. You can also:
Set
restricted: true
to hide a block from the block chooser (only usable as child blocks)
Set
mostUsed: true
to pin a block to the top of the chooser
Set
sidebarSchemaOnly: true
to use only the schema form in the sidebar (no custom edit component)
Use
fieldsets
in the schema to organize fields into tabs
HTML Annotations for Visual Editing

annotations-text.plaintext: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing.

children.text: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing:
data-block-uid="blockId"
— Click-to-select blocks. Hydra.js adds click handlers and shows a blue outline and Quanta toolbar on selected blocks.
data-edit-text="fieldName"
— Inline text editing. For simple text, click and type directly. For rich text (slate widget), select text to apply formatting via the Quanta toolbar.
data-edit-media="fieldName"
— Visual media uploading. Editors can upload, pick or drag-and-drop images directly onto the element.
data-edit-link="fieldName"
— Link editing. Click behaviour is replaced with a link picker to select content, enter an external URL, or open the link.
